categories.name: Afterlife
categories.description: Covers ancient, biblical, and Christian views of life after death, including resurrection, heaven and hell, eternal punishment, purgatory, Jewish afterlife traditions, and journeys to the realm of the dead.

categories.name: Ancient World and Social Context
categories.description: Provides historical background for biblical and early Christian texts, including Roman rule, Greco-Roman religious culture, literacy, archaeology, social class, wealth, crucifixion, and burial practices.

categories.name: Revelation and End-Times Interpretation
categories.description: Covers the book of Revelation and later end-times interpretations, including authorship, historical setting, symbolism, judgment, 666, the rapture, Armageddon, and modern uses of biblical prophecy.

categories.name: Authorship
categories.description: Examines who wrote biblical and early Christian texts, how anonymous writings received names, how sources were composed, and how ancient secretaries affect authorship claims.

categories.name: Bart's Books
categories.description: Covers Bart's major books, including announcements, excerpts, interviews, reader questions, reviews, responses to critics, and posts developing arguments from those books.

categories.name: Bible Translations and Versions
categories.description: Covers major Bible versions, translation issues, the Septuagint, the King James Version, the NRSV, and the process of rendering ancient biblical texts into modern languages.

categories.name: Blog Features and Personal Posts
categories.description: Covers recurring blog features, fundraising and charity updates, autobiographical posts, and personal reflections.

categories.name: Biblical Canon, Authority, and Interpretation
categories.description: Covers how writings became scripture, how biblical authority is defended or challenged, and how biblical texts are interpreted, harmonized, debated, or used as Christian scripture.

categories.name: Canonical Gospels and Acts
categories.description: Covers the canonical Gospels and Acts, including authorship, dating, sources, historical reliability, Synoptic relationships, distinctive literary features, and Acts as Luke's account of early Christianity.
topicOrder: Signs in the Gospel of John

categories.name: Christian Origins and Expansion
categories.description: Covers the beginnings, diversity, and growth of early Christianity, including conversion, community formation, and explanations for Christianity's spread in the Roman world.

categories.name: Christology
categories.description: Examines early Christian views of Christ's identity, divinity, relationship to God, exaltation, adoption, incarnation, angelic status, and portrayal in key texts.

categories.name: Church Fathers
categories.description: Covers the Apostolic Fathers and later early church writers, including Papias, Polycarp traditions, Eusebius, Athanasius, Didymus the Blind, Augustine, and writings or traditions associated with them.

categories.name: Early Church History
categories.description: Covers post-New-Testament church history, including early Christian writings, persecution, bishops, councils, Constantine, institutional change, and doctrines such as the Trinity.

categories.name: Early Judaism
categories.description: Covers Jewish groups, law, apocalyptic thought, practices, texts, and historical settings around the time of Jesus and early Christianity, including the Dead Sea Scrolls and Essenes.

categories.name: Epistles
categories.description: Covers New Testament epistles, including Pauline and non-Pauline letters, authorship, forgery, letter composition, persecution, and individual letters such as Philippians and 2 Thessalonians.

categories.name: Ethics and Moral Reasoning
categories.description: Covers moral reasoning in ancient, biblical, and Christian contexts, including Jesus' ethics, Paul and slavery, moral problems in scripture, and Christian anti-Judaism.

categories.name: Faith, Doubt, and Nonbelief
categories.description: Covers belief, doubt, agnosticism, atheism, deconversion, fundamentalism, and personal or intellectual reasons people lose, question, or revise faith.

categories.name: Forgery
categories.description: Covers ancient and modern forgery claims, pseudepigraphy, disputed attributions, and writings falsely or questionably connected with Paul, Peter, or other early Christian figures.

categories.name: Gnosticism, Orthodoxy, and Heresy
categories.description: Covers Gnostic movements, Nag Hammadi writings, Marcion, the Gospel of Judas, Mary Magdalene traditions, and early Christian disputes over doctrine, scripture, authority, orthodoxy, and heresy.

categories.name: Hebrew Bible Texts and Traditions
categories.description: Covers Hebrew Bible books and traditions, including authorship, sources, historical questions, manuscript transmission, translation, apocryphal writings, divine beings, and interpretation.
topicOrder: Divine Beings in the Hebrew Bible

categories.name: The Historical Jesus
categories.description: Covers historical reconstruction of Jesus' life and public activity, including his existence, apocalyptic message, messiahship, Son of Man sayings, Nazareth, Roman political context, and the methods scholars use to assess the evidence.
topicOrder: Methods for Studying the Historical Jesus

categories.name: Historical and Critical Methods
categories.description: Covers methods used to study biblical and early Christian texts historically, including dating, source criticism, redaction criticism, historical reliability, miracle claims, comparative evidence, and the relationship between history and theology.
topicOrder: Methods for Studying the Historical Jesus

categories.name: Jesus' Birth, Family, and Miracles
categories.description: Covers traditions about Jesus' birth, genealogy, family, possible marriage, relatives, and miracle stories in the canonical Gospels and related traditions.

categories.name: Jesus' Death, Burial, and Resurrection
categories.description: Covers Gospel passion narratives, Jesus' trial, crucifixion, burial, empty tomb traditions, resurrection appearances, apologetic debates, and early interpretations of Jesus' death and resurrection, including Roman crucifixion and burial practices.

categories.name: Jesus' Teachings and Social World
categories.description: Covers Jesus' teachings, ethical vision, economic setting, views on wealth and poverty, and traditions involving women in his life and Gospel portrayals.

categories.name: Manuscripts and Textual Evidence
categories.description: Covers ancient manuscript evidence, manuscript discoveries and controversies, dating methods, and major textual finds such as Codex Sinaiticus, the Dead Sea Scrolls, Papyrus Egerton, early New Testament manuscripts, and Quran fragments.

categories.name: Memory, Oral Tradition, and Eyewitnesses
categories.description: Covers oral transmission, memory, eyewitness testimony, and debates over how traditions about Jesus and the Gospels were preserved, reshaped, or invented before being written.

categories.name: Miracles and Supernatural Claims
categories.description: Covers miracle traditions, healings, exorcisms, visions, signs, miracle workers, apologetic arguments, and historical questions about supernatural claims in biblical and related texts.
topicOrder: Signs in the Gospel of John

categories.name: New Testament Figures
categories.description: Covers named figures in and around the New Testament narratives, including Jesus' family, followers, opponents, and Roman authorities.

categories.name: Non-Canonical Christian Texts
categories.description: Covers early Christian writings outside the New Testament, including non-canonical gospels, infancy traditions, apocalypses, apocryphal acts, church manuals, apostolic writings, miracle stories, and manuscript fragments.

categories.name: Paul's Life, Mission, and Communities
categories.description: Covers Paul's life, conversion, missionary activity, conflicts, persecution, churches, portrayal in Acts, and what Paul appears to have known about Jesus.

categories.name: Pauline Letters and Textual Questions
categories.description: Covers Paul's letters, including disputed authorship, Pauline forgeries, composite letters, textual variants, original-text problems, Philippians, 2 Thessalonians, and the transmission of Pauline writings.

categories.name: Pauline Thought and Practice
categories.description: Covers Paul's thought and practice, including salvation, crucifixion, resurrection, Jewish law, apocalyptic expectation, slavery, women, ethics, and the Philippians 2 Christ poem.

categories.name: Persecution and Martyrdom
categories.description: Covers persecution and martyrdom traditions, including Roman persecution, New Testament letters, Paul as persecutor and persecuted, apostolic death traditions, Perpetua, Polycarp, and miraculous martyrdom traditions.

categories.name: Salvation, Atonement, and Judgment
categories.description: Covers salvation, atonement, divine judgment, eternal punishment, free will, predestination, and distinctive Pauline or Luke-Acts interpretations of salvation and Jesus' death.

categories.name: Academic Life, Scholars, and Public Debate
categories.description: Covers academic life, professional research, scholarly publishing, major scholars, memorials, and public debates.

categories.name: Suffering and Evil
categories.description: Covers suffering and evil as theological, biblical, philosophical, and personal problems, including disasters, free will, Job, biblical explanations of suffering, and suffering as a challenge to faith.

categories.name: Teaching, Books, and Media
categories.description: Covers teaching, courses, textbooks, general-audience books, writing and publishing, media appearances, media coverage, and public-facing scholarship.

categories.name: Textual Criticism
categories.description: Covers how scholars compare manuscripts and other textual witnesses to evaluate variants, scribal changes, original-text questions, patristic evidence, Pauline textual issues, and doctrinally significant readings.

categories.name: Wealth, Poverty, and Charity
categories.description: Covers posts on charity, altruism, care for the poor, ancient and Christian views of wealth, and moral obligations toward people in need.

categories.name: Women, Gender, and Sexuality
categories.description: Covers women in Jesus, Pauline, and early Christian traditions, along with biblical debates about gender, sexuality, same-sex relations, and reproductive ethics.
